summary_one_line: Input must carry a well-formed TLV protocol header (pop3 + host:port + response TLVs) so curl opens a socket and select() times out (rc==0).

hypotheses.what_input_feature: A structured fuzz-corpus TLV stream whose leading record encodes a recognized curl protocol (literal `pop3` at offset 0x06) followed by a parseable host:port (`...0.1:9001`) and one or more response TLV records, so that fuzz_handle_transfer drives a connection where a socket is FUZZ_SOCK_OPEN and fuzz_select returns 0 (timed out) — the rc==0 true side at line 385.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: Winner-resolving seeds carry the exact protocol/host literals: seed 1 has `pop3` at 0x06 and a `:::::::900` host:port run, seeds 2-4 carry `pop3` plus `0.1:9001` and `GHTTP/`-style response TLV bytes; W bytes at offsets 0x06-0x1d are tightly concentrated (e.g. `70(p)x4`, `3a(:)x4`, `39(9)x4`, `30(0)x4`) showing all four winners agree on the host:port literal, and the W-only overlay lights up lines 386-400 (the timeout block) which the loser never reaches.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: Loser-blocking seeds lack the coherent protocol header — they are dominated by `%A`, `CD`, `..` and `Y`-run garbage (seed 1: `%A2%77%dCD`, seed 5: `YYYY?YYYZZZ`), the same offsets that are single-valued in W are scattered in L (offset 0x06 is `2e(.)x3 70(p)x2 00(.)x1 73(s)x1`, 0x16 is `25(%)x2 43(C)x1 6f(o)x1 59(Y)x1`), so curl never opens a socket and select takes the rc!=0 false side — the L overlay shows line 385 with `T=0 F=7`.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: cmplog/vpc run I2SRandReplace: the CmpLogObserver records the operands of the TLV-magic and protocol-string comparisons inside curl's fuzz harness and curl's protocol dispatch, then I2SRandReplace scans the input and substitutes the matching operand verbatim, planting the `pop3`/host:port literals that naive and value_profile must stumble onto by chance. The dash-row I2S-floor ancestors in both winners' lineage confirm an I2S find in the chain.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocked true side is the rc==0 (select timed out) branch in fuzz_handle_transfer.
rc is the return of fuzz_select, so the rc==0 side depends on an open socket plus a select timeout.
Winner-resolving seeds carry the literal protocol token pop3 and a host:port.
Winner seeds carry a recognizable host:port run.
The W-only overlay lights the timeout block lines 386-400 that the loser never reaches.
Across the four winners the host:port literal offsets are single-valued (concentrated).
evidence_trail.cited_section: BYTE DIFF (W vs L at common offsets)

evidence_trail.claim: Loser seeds lack the coherent header and carry percent/garbage runs instead.
At the blocker line the loser takes the false side exclusively (T=0 F=7).
evidence_trail.exact_quote: d=1   L 385  T=8 F=4  T=0 F=7  else if(rc == 0) {  <-- BLOCKER

evidence_trail.claim: cmplog/vpc add I2SRandReplace which substitutes a recorded CMP operand at matching input offsets.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
scans the input for byte sequences matching one
operand, and substitutes the other operand at those offsets.

evidence_trail.claim: value_profile lacks I2SRandReplace, so the loser pair member cannot plant the operand directly.

mechanism_consistency_check.verification_method: Ran `python3 tools/db_query.py lineage --branch 567 --role W --fuzzer value_profile_cmplog --trial 1 --seed 5d9ce7f391be0a62` and observed I2S-floor dash rows (mutation_op = -) at depth 19 and 20; also ran the cmplog winner faf6b58d07527f1a (trial 2), which has an I2S-floor dash row at depth 22. Both winning chains contain unwrapped I2SRandReplace ancestors.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness gating rc==0 only on a non-comparison input property (e.g. total input length) — if cmplog/vpc show no advantage over naive/value_profile there, the I2S-operand-substitution attribution is wrong.

weakest_evidence_point: The exact CMP whose operand I2S substitutes (curl protocol-dispatch string vs harness TLV magic) is inferred from seed bytes, not pinned to a specific intercepted comparison in the cov dump.
